questions.prompt: Which of the following best explains why Mississippian societies such as Cahokia developed dense urban populations by 1100 CE?
choices: Adoption of intensive maize agriculture and floodplain cultivation
Transatlantic trade with West African empires
Conquest by Mesoamerican migrants from the Valley of Mexico
Conversion to Christianity by Norse missionaries
questions.correctText: Adoption of intensive maize agriculture and floodplain cultivation

questions.prompt: The Iroquois Confederacy's Great Law of Peace is most significant for demonstrating that pre-Columbian Native societies
choices: Possessed sophisticated political institutions including federated governance
Were dependent on European mediation to resolve intertribal disputes
Rejected hereditary leadership in favor of pure democracy
Practiced patriarchal succession identical to European monarchies
questions.correctText: Possessed sophisticated political institutions including federated governance

questions.prompt: The Columbian Exchange most directly contributed to which long-term demographic change?
choices: Collapse of Native populations from epidemic disease and rapid European population growth from New World crops
Mass European migration to Mesoamerica before 1550
Decline of African populations due to maize cultivation
Stabilization of indigenous populations through smallpox inoculation
questions.correctText: Collapse of Native populations from epidemic disease and rapid European population growth from New World crops

questions.prompt: The Spanish encomienda system in the sixteenth century is best characterized as
choices: A coerced labor regime granting Spanish settlers tribute and labor from indigenous communities
An egalitarian land-grant program for free Native peasants
A church-administered missionary system without economic exploitation
A self-governing council system for indigenous nobility
questions.correctText: A coerced labor regime granting Spanish settlers tribute and labor from indigenous communities

questions.prompt: The Spanish casta system most clearly reflected which colonial priority?
choices: Maintaining social hierarchy through racial classification and limited mobility
Promoting equality between Iberians and indigenous peoples
Encouraging intermarriage to dissolve ethnic distinctions
Eliminating African presence in colonial society
questions.correctText: Maintaining social hierarchy through racial classification and limited mobility

questions.prompt: Compared to Spanish colonization, French and Dutch colonization of North America was distinguished primarily by
choices: A focus on fur-trade alliances with Native peoples rather than large settler populations
Mass conversion of Algonquian peoples to Catholicism by Dutch missionaries
Plantation slavery on the scale of the Chesapeake
Permanent ethnic cleansing of all indigenous communities
questions.correctText: A focus on fur-trade alliances with Native peoples rather than large settler populations

questions.prompt: The Pueblo Revolt of 1680 is most significant because it demonstrated that
choices: Indigenous peoples could successfully resist and temporarily expel European colonizers
Spanish missionaries had completed full conversion of the Pueblo peoples
Apache and Pueblo nations united permanently against Spain
European diseases had effectively destroyed organized Pueblo resistance
questions.correctText: Indigenous peoples could successfully resist and temporarily expel European colonizers

questions.prompt: The 'starving time' at Jamestown in 1609-1610 most directly resulted from which combination of factors?
choices: Reliance on gold-seeking gentlemen rather than farmers and conflict with Powhatan peoples
Heavy Quaker influence on labor norms
An overproduction of tobacco that flooded the colony
Strict adherence to mercantilist export quotas
questions.correctText: Reliance on gold-seeking gentlemen rather than farmers and conflict with Powhatan peoples

questions.prompt: The first meeting of the Virginia House of Burgesses in 1619 is historically significant primarily because it
choices: Established representative self-government in English North America
Abolished slavery in the Chesapeake region
Created a state-supported Anglican church for the colony
Implemented the Navigation Acts in Virginia
questions.correctText: Established representative self-government in English North America

questions.prompt: The Mayflower Compact of 1620 is best described as
choices: A covenant establishing self-government through mutual consent of the governed
A royal charter granted by King James I to the Plymouth settlers
A trade agreement between the Pilgrims and Wampanoag Confederacy
A religious doctrine rejecting Calvinist predestination
questions.correctText: A covenant establishing self-government through mutual consent of the governed

questions.prompt: John Winthrop's 'City Upon a Hill' sermon (1630) most clearly expressed the Puritan belief that
choices: Massachusetts Bay was to serve as a model Christian community visible to the world
Colonists should pursue religious toleration of all Christian denominations
Government should remain entirely separate from church authority
Material wealth was inherently sinful and should be redistributed
questions.correctText: Massachusetts Bay was to serve as a model Christian community visible to the world

questions.prompt: Roger Williams and Anne Hutchinson were both banished from Massachusetts Bay primarily because they
choices: Challenged Puritan orthodoxy and the union of church and civil authority
Advocated armed rebellion against the colonial governor
Refused to participate in the fur trade with the Narragansett
Insisted on uniform Anglican worship
questions.correctText: Challenged Puritan orthodoxy and the union of church and civil authority

questions.prompt: Bacon's Rebellion of 1676 is most often cited as a turning point because it
choices: Accelerated the Chesapeake's shift from indentured servitude to racialized slavery
Established the first permanent peace between Virginia and the Susquehannock
Ended the influence of the Anglican Church in Virginia
Resulted in immediate independence from Britain
questions.correctText: Accelerated the Chesapeake's shift from indentured servitude to racialized slavery

questions.prompt: British mercantilism, embodied in the Navigation Acts, was designed primarily to
choices: Channel colonial trade and resources to benefit the imperial economy
Promote free trade between colonies and continental Europe
Encourage colonial manufacturing of finished goods
Ban all importation of African enslaved labor
questions.correctText: Channel colonial trade and resources to benefit the imperial economy

questions.prompt: Jonathan Edwards's sermon 'Sinners in the Hands of an Angry God' (1741) is best understood as
choices: An expression of the First Great Awakening's emotional revivalism
A defense of Anglican rationalism against dissenters
A repudiation of Calvinist doctrines of human depravity
A political pamphlet calling for independence from Britain
questions.correctText: An expression of the First Great Awakening's emotional revivalism

questions.prompt: The French and Indian War (1754-1763) most directly contributed to the imperial crisis by
choices: Producing British debt and new colonial taxes alongside the Proclamation of 1763
Eliminating France as a North American power and ending colonial complaint
Strengthening colonial loyalty to Parliament through shared victory
Granting colonies new representation in the House of Commons
questions.correctText: Producing British debt and new colonial taxes alongside the Proclamation of 1763

questions.prompt: The Albany Plan of Union (1754) is significant primarily because it
choices: Foreshadowed later attempts at intercolonial cooperation and federal union
Established a permanent military alliance with the Iroquois
Created the first standing colonial army under Benjamin Franklin
Imposed direct British taxation on the colonies
questions.correctText: Foreshadowed later attempts at intercolonial cooperation and federal union

questions.prompt: Colonial opposition to the Stamp Act (1765) rested most directly on the principle that
choices: Only colonial assemblies could levy internal taxes on colonists
Parliament lacked authority to regulate imperial trade
External duties were inherently unconstitutional
Colonists deserved seats in the House of Lords
questions.correctText: Only colonial assemblies could levy internal taxes on colonists

questions.prompt: Thomas Paine's 'Common Sense' (1776) most effectively persuaded American readers by
choices: Using plain language to attack monarchy and argue for independence
Detailing the constitutional history of English common law
Defending reconciliation with Parliament under new terms
Endorsing a constitutional monarchy modeled on Britain
questions.correctText: Using plain language to attack monarchy and argue for independence

questions.prompt: The Declaration of Independence drew most heavily on which Enlightenment philosopher's ideas?
choices: John Locke's theories of natural rights and consent of the governed
Thomas Hobbes's defense of absolute sovereignty
Adam Smith's theory of comparative advantage
Jean-Jacques Rousseau's concept of the noble savage
questions.correctText: John Locke's theories of natural rights and consent of the governed

questions.prompt: The Articles of Confederation's most fundamental weakness was that they
choices: Denied Congress the power to tax or regulate interstate commerce
Allowed unrestricted federal taxation of state governments
Created a strong executive with veto authority
Failed to address western land claims
questions.correctText: Denied Congress the power to tax or regulate interstate commerce

questions.prompt: The Northwest Ordinance of 1787 is significant because it
choices: Created an orderly process for territorial admission as new states and banned slavery in the Northwest Territory
Permitted slavery in all territories north of the Ohio River
Established the first national bank under the Articles
Granted statehood automatically to Vermont and Kentucky
questions.correctText: Created an orderly process for territorial admission as new states and banned slavery in the Northwest Territory

questions.prompt: Shays' Rebellion (1786-87) most directly influenced the Constitutional Convention by
choices: Convincing many leaders that a stronger central government was needed
Demonstrating the success of state militias under the Articles
Persuading delegates to expand state sovereignty further
Ending debates over representation in Congress
questions.correctText: Convincing many leaders that a stronger central government was needed

questions.prompt: The Three-Fifths Compromise at the Constitutional Convention resolved which conflict?
choices: Disagreement over how enslaved persons would be counted for representation and taxation
Tension between large and small states over the Senate
A dispute over the location of the national capital
Debate over slavery's expansion into the Northwest Territory
questions.correctText: Disagreement over how enslaved persons would be counted for representation and taxation

questions.prompt: Madison's argument in Federalist No. 10 is best summarized as the claim that
choices: A large republic better controls the dangers of faction by diluting their influence
Direct democracy is the safest form of government
Factions can be eliminated by suppressing minority rights
States must remain sovereign to prevent tyranny
questions.correctText: A large republic better controls the dangers of faction by diluting their influence

questions.prompt: Anti-Federalists opposed ratification of the Constitution primarily because they feared
choices: A consolidated national government would threaten state sovereignty and individual liberty
The new government would be too weak to defend against Britain
The proposed Bill of Rights expanded federal power excessively
The Constitution prohibited any future amendments
questions.correctText: A consolidated national government would threaten state sovereignty and individual liberty

questions.prompt: The Virginia and Kentucky Resolutions of 1798-99 introduced which constitutional doctrine?
choices: State nullification of federal laws deemed unconstitutional
Judicial review by the Supreme Court
Implied powers of Congress under the necessary and proper clause
Federal supremacy over state legislatures
questions.correctText: State nullification of federal laws deemed unconstitutional

questions.prompt: Marbury v. Madison (1803) established the precedent of
choices: Judicial review of federal legislation by the Supreme Court
Federal supremacy over state criminal law
Congressional authority over executive branch appointments
Direct election of senators by the people
questions.correctText: Judicial review of federal legislation by the Supreme Court

questions.prompt: The Louisiana Purchase (1803) created a constitutional dilemma for Jefferson because
choices: The Constitution did not explicitly authorize the executive to acquire foreign territory
Federalists controlled the Senate and opposed expansion
France demanded that slavery be banned in the territory
Britain claimed competing rights to the Mississippi Valley
questions.correctText: The Constitution did not explicitly authorize the executive to acquire foreign territory

questions.prompt: The 'Era of Good Feelings' under James Monroe is best characterized by
choices: Single-party dominance combined with deepening sectional tensions
Genuine national unity on slavery and tariffs
A formal alliance with Great Britain
The collapse of the two-party system permanently
questions.correctText: Single-party dominance combined with deepening sectional tensions

questions.prompt: The Missouri Compromise (1820) most directly addressed
choices: The expansion of slavery into the Louisiana Purchase territory
Federal funding for internal improvements such as canals
Tariff rates on imported cotton textiles
Treaty obligations with Spain over Florida
questions.correctText: The expansion of slavery into the Louisiana Purchase territory

questions.prompt: The Monroe Doctrine (1823) declared that the United States would
choices: Oppose European intervention in the Western Hemisphere
Annex Cuba and Puerto Rico from Spain
Withdraw from all European trade
Recognize British naval supremacy in the Caribbean
questions.correctText: Oppose European intervention in the Western Hemisphere

questions.prompt: The Market Revolution of the early nineteenth century was characterized by all of the following EXCEPT
choices: Decline of regional specialization in agriculture
Construction of canals such as the Erie Canal
Growth of textile manufacturing in Lowell-style mills
Spread of the telegraph and improved communication
questions.correctText: Decline of regional specialization in agriculture

questions.prompt: The Second Great Awakening most directly fueled which set of antebellum reform movements?
choices: Abolitionism, temperance, women's rights, and public education reform
Pro-slavery defenses of paternalism in the Deep South
Manifest Destiny expansion into Mexico
Industrial labor unions in Massachusetts
questions.correctText: Abolitionism, temperance, women's rights, and public education reform

questions.prompt: William Lloyd Garrison's 'The Liberator' is best understood as advocating
choices: Immediate, uncompensated emancipation of enslaved people
Gradual colonization of free Black Americans to Africa
Compensated emancipation supported by federal taxes
States' rights to determine slavery's future
questions.correctText: Immediate, uncompensated emancipation of enslaved people

questions.prompt: The Seneca Falls Convention of 1848 produced which foundational document?
choices: The Declaration of Sentiments demanding women's equality
The Declaration of Independence rewritten for laborers
The Liberator's manifesto on immediate abolition
The Treaty of Guadalupe Hidalgo
questions.correctText: The Declaration of Sentiments demanding women's equality

questions.prompt: The Indian Removal Act of 1830 most directly resulted in
choices: The forced relocation of southeastern Native nations along the Trail of Tears
Voluntary western migration of the Iroquois Confederacy
A federal ban on Native sovereignty in the Northwest
The end of treaty-making with Indigenous peoples
questions.correctText: The forced relocation of southeastern Native nations along the Trail of Tears

questions.prompt: Andrew Jackson's veto of the Bank of the United States recharter (1832) reflected his belief that
choices: The Bank concentrated power in a wealthy elite at the expense of ordinary citizens
A national bank was constitutionally required for prosperity
Federal banking should expand to compete with state banks
Northern industrialists needed greater federal support
questions.correctText: The Bank concentrated power in a wealthy elite at the expense of ordinary citizens

questions.prompt: The Nullification Crisis of 1832-33 centered on South Carolina's claim that it could
choices: Void federal tariff laws within state borders
Withdraw from the Constitution at will
Abolish slavery without federal approval
Maintain a separate currency from the Union
questions.correctText: Void federal tariff laws within state borders

questions.prompt: The ideology of Manifest Destiny was most often used in the 1840s to justify
choices: U.S. territorial expansion across the North American continent
Federal protection of Native American sovereignty
Limiting slavery to the original southern states
Pacifist diplomacy with Mexico
questions.correctText: U.S. territorial expansion across the North American continent

questions.prompt: The Wilmot Proviso (1846) most directly contributed to sectional conflict by proposing to
choices: Prohibit slavery in any territory acquired from Mexico
Extend slavery into all western territories
Repeal the Missouri Compromise line
Abolish slavery throughout the United States
questions.correctText: Prohibit slavery in any territory acquired from Mexico

questions.prompt: The Compromise of 1850 included which provision that most outraged northern abolitionists?
choices: A strengthened Fugitive Slave Act requiring northern cooperation in returning escaped slaves
Banning slavery in all territories north of 36 degrees 30 minutes
Immediate abolition of slavery in Washington, D.C.
Federal compensation for emancipated slaves
questions.correctText: A strengthened Fugitive Slave Act requiring northern cooperation in returning escaped slaves

questions.prompt: Harriet Beecher Stowe's 'Uncle Tom's Cabin' (1852) most directly contributed to sectional tensions by
choices: Building northern moral opposition to slavery through emotional narrative
Justifying southern paternalism with biblical citations
Defending the Fugitive Slave Act in popular fiction
Encouraging Christian acceptance of slavery as natural
questions.correctText: Building northern moral opposition to slavery through emotional narrative

questions.prompt: The Kansas-Nebraska Act of 1854 most directly inflamed sectional conflict because it
choices: Repealed the Missouri Compromise and opened territories to popular sovereignty over slavery
Banned slavery permanently north of the Mason-Dixon Line
Required Kansas to immediately admit slavery
Forbade settlement in the Nebraska Territory
questions.correctText: Repealed the Missouri Compromise and opened territories to popular sovereignty over slavery

questions.prompt: The Supreme Court's decision in Dred Scott v. Sandford (1857) ruled that
choices: African Americans were not citizens and Congress could not ban slavery in the territories
The Missouri Compromise remained constitutional
Popular sovereignty alone could determine slavery's expansion
Enslaved persons gained freedom by entering free states
questions.correctText: African Americans were not citizens and Congress could not ban slavery in the territories

questions.prompt: John Brown's raid on Harpers Ferry (1859) deepened sectional conflict because it
choices: Convinced southerners that abolitionists endorsed violent insurrection
Persuaded northern Democrats to support secession
Ended slavery in Virginia immediately
Was secretly funded by President Buchanan
questions.correctText: Convinced southerners that abolitionists endorsed violent insurrection

questions.prompt: Abraham Lincoln's election in November 1860 most directly triggered
choices: The secession of South Carolina and the formation of the Confederacy
An immediate constitutional amendment abolishing slavery
The Dred Scott decision by the Supreme Court
Passage of the Wilmot Proviso through Congress
questions.correctText: The secession of South Carolina and the formation of the Confederacy

questions.prompt: The Union victory at Antietam (1862) is historically significant primarily because it
choices: Provided Lincoln the opportunity to issue the Emancipation Proclamation
Ended the Civil War with Confederate surrender
Resulted in the recognition of the Confederacy by Britain
Permitted Sherman's March to the Sea
questions.correctText: Provided Lincoln the opportunity to issue the Emancipation Proclamation

questions.prompt: The simultaneous Union victories at Vicksburg and Gettysburg in July 1863 are significant because they
choices: Marked a strategic turning point that shifted momentum to the Union
Forced immediate Confederate surrender at Appomattox
Ended the use of the Anaconda Plan by Union forces
Persuaded France to recognize the Confederacy
questions.correctText: Marked a strategic turning point that shifted momentum to the Union

questions.prompt: The 13th Amendment (1865), 14th Amendment (1868), and 15th Amendment (1870) together represent
choices: The constitutional foundations of Reconstruction-era civil and political rights
Federal recognition of states' rights to regulate citizenship
A repudiation of the Emancipation Proclamation
Restrictions on federal power during Reconstruction
questions.correctText: The constitutional foundations of Reconstruction-era civil and political rights

questions.prompt: Radical Republicans and Andrew Johnson clashed most fundamentally over
choices: Whether Reconstruction should impose strict requirements on the former Confederacy
The wartime use of the suspension of habeas corpus
Whether the Emancipation Proclamation should be repealed
The deployment of the Freedmen's Bureau to northern states
questions.correctText: Whether Reconstruction should impose strict requirements on the former Confederacy

questions.prompt: Sharecropping in the postwar South most closely resembled which of the following labor systems?
choices: A form of debt peonage that bound formerly enslaved laborers to landowners
Yeoman farming on independently owned homesteads
Industrial wage labor as practiced in New England factories
Subsistence agriculture practiced by Native peoples
questions.correctText: A form of debt peonage that bound formerly enslaved laborers to landowners

questions.prompt: The Compromise of 1877 most directly resulted in
choices: The withdrawal of federal troops from the South and the end of Reconstruction
The peaceful integration of southern schools
Federal enforcement of the 15th Amendment in the Deep South
A constitutional amendment guaranteeing women's suffrage
questions.correctText: The withdrawal of federal troops from the South and the end of Reconstruction

questions.prompt: Andrew Carnegie's 'Gospel of Wealth' (1889) argued that
choices: Wealthy industrialists had a moral duty to use their fortunes for public benefit
Government should redistribute wealth to alleviate poverty
Labor unions deserved a share of industrial profits
Social Darwinism justified eliminating welfare entirely
questions.correctText: Wealthy industrialists had a moral duty to use their fortunes for public benefit

questions.prompt: Standard Oil's horizontal integration strategy most directly enabled it to
choices: Eliminate competitors and dominate the refining sector of the petroleum industry
Manufacture every stage of oil production from drilling to retail
Diversify into railroads and shipping under one corporation
Acquire foreign oil fields under federal protection
questions.correctText: Eliminate competitors and dominate the refining sector of the petroleum industry

questions.prompt: The Sherman Antitrust Act of 1890 was designed to
choices: Prohibit combinations in restraint of interstate trade
Establish the first national income tax
Regulate child labor in interstate commerce
Recognize the legal right to strike
questions.correctText: Prohibit combinations in restraint of interstate trade

questions.prompt: Samuel Gompers's American Federation of Labor differed from the Knights of Labor primarily in its
choices: Focus on skilled craft workers and 'bread-and-butter' issues such as wages and hours
Acceptance of women, African Americans, and unskilled laborers in one union
Advocacy of immediate socialist revolution
Rejection of collective bargaining entirely
questions.correctText: Focus on skilled craft workers and 'bread-and-butter' issues such as wages and hours
questions.topic: Period 6: AFL vs Knights of Labor

questions.prompt: The Haymarket Affair (1886), Homestead Strike (1892), and Pullman Strike (1894) each illustrate
choices: The federal government's tendency to side with employers against organized labor
Successful union victories during the Gilded Age
The decline of public sympathy for industrialists
The growing power of the Knights of Labor
questions.correctText: The federal government's tendency to side with employers against organized labor

questions.prompt: Jane Addams's Hull House in Chicago is best understood as part of which Gilded Age response to urbanization?
choices: The settlement house movement and Social Gospel reform
The temperance crusade led by the Women's Christian Temperance Union
The Populist Party's farmer alliance
The Sherman Antitrust enforcement campaign
questions.correctText: The settlement house movement and Social Gospel reform

questions.prompt: The Dawes Severalty Act of 1887 sought to
choices: Assimilate Native Americans by breaking up tribal landholdings into individual plots
Restore tribal sovereignty over reservation lands
Protect Plains Indian buffalo populations
Grant Native Americans full voting rights
questions.correctText: Assimilate Native Americans by breaking up tribal landholdings into individual plots

questions.prompt: The Populist movement of the 1890s most strongly drew its support from
choices: Western and southern farmers facing falling prices and railroad monopolies
Northeastern industrial workers in textile mills
Wealthy urban industrialists supporting tariff reform
Plains Indian tribes resisting allotment
questions.correctText: Western and southern farmers facing falling prices and railroad monopolies

questions.prompt: William Jennings Bryan's 'Cross of Gold' speech (1896) advocated
choices: The free coinage of silver to inflate the currency and aid debtors
A return to the gold standard for international stability
Federal income taxation on corporations
Government ownership of all railroads
questions.correctText: The free coinage of silver to inflate the currency and aid debtors
questions.topic: Period 6: Bryan and Cross of Gold

questions.prompt: The Supreme Court's decision in Plessy v. Ferguson (1896) established the doctrine that
choices: Separate but equal facilities were constitutionally permissible
All state laws mandating segregation violated the 14th Amendment
Federal civil rights legislation applied equally to private businesses
Public schools could not be racially segregated
questions.correctText: Separate but equal facilities were constitutionally permissible

questions.prompt: The Spanish-American War of 1898 is most often interpreted by historians as a pivotal moment in U.S. history because it
choices: Marked the United States's emergence as an overseas imperial power
Returned Cuba and the Philippines to Spanish control
Ended American interest in Pacific expansion
Inaugurated a policy of strict neutrality in foreign affairs
questions.correctText: Marked the United States's emergence as an overseas imperial power

questions.prompt: Upton Sinclair's 'The Jungle' (1906) most directly led to the passage of
choices: The Pure Food and Drug Act and Meat Inspection Act
The Sherman Antitrust Act
The Sixteenth Amendment
The Federal Reserve Act
questions.correctText: The Pure Food and Drug Act and Meat Inspection Act

questions.prompt: Theodore Roosevelt's 'Square Deal' is best characterized as
choices: A reform program balancing the interests of consumers, workers, and business
An exclusive defense of large corporations against regulation
A return to laissez-faire economic policy
A repudiation of conservation
questions.correctText: A reform program balancing the interests of consumers, workers, and business

questions.prompt: The 19th Amendment, ratified in 1920, accomplished which goal long advocated by reformers?
choices: Granting women the constitutional right to vote
Establishing federal prohibition of alcohol
Outlawing child labor in interstate commerce
Direct election of senators
questions.correctText: Granting women the constitutional right to vote

questions.prompt: The founding of the NAACP in 1909 reflected
choices: A shift toward legal challenges to segregation and disenfranchisement
Booker T. Washington's strategy of vocational education
Marcus Garvey's Pan-African nationalism
A turn toward armed resistance against Jim Crow
questions.correctText: A shift toward legal challenges to segregation and disenfranchisement

questions.prompt: Wilson's Fourteen Points (1918) most directly proposed
choices: Self-determination of peoples and a League of Nations to prevent future wars
Annexation of the Rhineland by France
American withdrawal from all overseas commitments
A revival of secret European alliances
questions.correctText: Self-determination of peoples and a League of Nations to prevent future wars

questions.prompt: The U.S. Senate's rejection of the Treaty of Versailles is best explained by
choices: Concerns that Article X of the League Covenant infringed on congressional war powers
Wilson's refusal to recognize American territorial gains in Europe
Republican support for unlimited free trade
Public outrage at the Fourteen Points
questions.correctText: Concerns that Article X of the League Covenant infringed on congressional war powers

questions.prompt: The Red Scare of 1919-1920 was characterized by
choices: Federal raids on suspected radicals and the deportation of foreign-born activists
A government endorsement of the Communist Party of America
Repeal of the Espionage and Sedition Acts
Mass naturalization of European immigrants
questions.correctText: Federal raids on suspected radicals and the deportation of foreign-born activists

questions.prompt: The Harlem Renaissance of the 1920s is best understood as
choices: A cultural flowering of African American literature, art, and music
A political movement for African colonization
A religious revival rooted in southern Pentecostalism
A migration of Black Americans to the rural South
questions.correctText: A cultural flowering of African American literature, art, and music

questions.prompt: The Scopes Trial (1925) most directly reflected
choices: Tensions between fundamentalist religion and modernist science in 1920s America
The end of public school education in Tennessee
Federal endorsement of evolutionary biology
A successful prosecution under the Espionage Act
questions.correctText: Tensions between fundamentalist religion and modernist science in 1920s America

questions.prompt: The stock market crash of October 1929 contributed to the Great Depression most directly by
choices: Triggering bank failures, deflation, and a collapse of consumer demand
Forcing the immediate passage of the New Deal
Producing the Dust Bowl drought in the Plains
Causing the collapse of the gold standard worldwide
questions.correctText: Triggering bank failures, deflation, and a collapse of consumer demand

questions.prompt: FDR's 'Hundred Days' (1933) is significant primarily because it
choices: Produced a rapid burst of New Deal legislation aimed at relief, recovery, and reform
Repealed the income tax to stimulate consumer spending
Eliminated the Federal Reserve System
Established the United Nations
questions.correctText: Produced a rapid burst of New Deal legislation aimed at relief, recovery, and reform

questions.prompt: The Social Security Act of 1935 most fundamentally transformed the federal government by
choices: Establishing a permanent federal commitment to old-age and unemployment insurance
Nationalizing the banking system permanently
Creating direct federal aid to public schools
Mandating universal national health insurance
questions.correctText: Establishing a permanent federal commitment to old-age and unemployment insurance

questions.prompt: FDR's court-packing plan of 1937 is best understood as
choices: An attempt to expand the Supreme Court to secure New Deal-friendly rulings
A successful constitutional amendment limiting judicial review
A repudiation of executive power
A bipartisan effort to limit presidential terms
questions.correctText: An attempt to expand the Supreme Court to secure New Deal-friendly rulings

questions.prompt: Japanese American internment during World War II was upheld by the Supreme Court in
choices: Korematsu v. United States (1944)
Plessy v. Ferguson (1896)
Brown v. Board of Education (1954)
Schenck v. United States (1919)
questions.correctText: Korematsu v. United States (1944)

questions.prompt: The Manhattan Project's most significant historical consequence was the
choices: Development of atomic weapons that ended the Pacific War and shaped the Cold War
Successful Allied invasion of North Africa
Eradication of polio in the postwar United States
Construction of the interstate highway system
questions.correctText: Development of atomic weapons that ended the Pacific War and shaped the Cold War

questions.prompt: The Truman Doctrine of 1947 most clearly established
choices: A U.S. policy of containment of Soviet expansion through aid to threatened nations
A formal military alliance with the Soviet Union
American withdrawal from European affairs
A return to interwar isolationism
questions.correctText: A U.S. policy of containment of Soviet expansion through aid to threatened nations

questions.prompt: The Marshall Plan (1948) provided economic aid to Western Europe primarily to
choices: Promote economic recovery and resist communist political appeal
Reward wartime neutrals in Scandinavia
Build up Soviet satellite economies
Restore the gold standard
questions.correctText: Promote economic recovery and resist communist political appeal

questions.prompt: McCarthyism in the early 1950s is best characterized as
choices: A politically charged campaign to expose alleged communist subversion through unsubstantiated accusations
A bipartisan defense of due process in loyalty investigations
A grassroots civil rights movement
A peace movement opposing the Korean War
questions.correctText: A politically charged campaign to expose alleged communist subversion through unsubstantiated accusations

questions.prompt: The Supreme Court's decision in Brown v. Board of Education (1954) overturned
choices: The 'separate but equal' doctrine in public education established by Plessy v. Ferguson
Korematsu v. United States and Japanese American internment
The constitutionality of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
The use of the necessary and proper clause
questions.correctText: The 'separate but equal' doctrine in public education established by Plessy v. Ferguson

questions.prompt: Martin Luther King Jr.'s 'Letter from Birmingham Jail' (1963) argued that
choices: Nonviolent direct action and civil disobedience were justified responses to unjust laws
Black Americans should embrace separatist self-defense
Federal legislation alone could end segregation
Religion should be excluded from political activism
questions.correctText: Nonviolent direct action and civil disobedience were justified responses to unjust laws

questions.prompt: The Civil Rights Act of 1964 and Voting Rights Act of 1965 together represent the federal government's most aggressive use of power to
choices: Ban discrimination in public accommodations, employment, and voting
Enforce restrictive immigration quotas
Protect Jim Crow segregation in southern states
Restore states' rights over civil rights enforcement
questions.correctText: Ban discrimination in public accommodations, employment, and voting
questions.topic: Period 8: Civil Rights Act and VRA

questions.prompt: Compared to Martin Luther King Jr., Malcolm X and the later Black Power movement emphasized
choices: Self-determination, racial pride, and the right of self-defense
Strict adherence to nonviolent integration
Reconciliation with white moderates
Reliance on federal courts alone to achieve change
questions.correctText: Self-determination, racial pride, and the right of self-defense
questions.topic: Period 8: Black Power and Malcolm X

questions.prompt: The Cuban Missile Crisis of October 1962 most directly resulted in
choices: A negotiated U.S.-Soviet de-escalation and a new emphasis on arms-control diplomacy
An invasion of Cuba by U.S. forces
The dissolution of NATO
Soviet recognition of West Germany
questions.correctText: A negotiated U.S.-Soviet de-escalation and a new emphasis on arms-control diplomacy

questions.prompt: Lyndon Johnson's Great Society programs most significantly expanded the federal welfare state by creating
choices: Medicare and Medicaid in 1965
Social Security in 1935
The Federal Reserve System in 1913
The CCC in 1933
questions.correctText: Medicare and Medicaid in 1965

questions.prompt: The Immigration and Nationality Act of 1965 transformed U.S. immigration by
choices: Ending national origins quotas and prioritizing family reunification
Reaffirming the 1924 quota system
Banning all Asian immigration permanently
Imposing literacy tests on European immigrants
questions.correctText: Ending national origins quotas and prioritizing family reunification

questions.prompt: The Tet Offensive of 1968 is significant because it
choices: Eroded American public support for the Vietnam War despite being a tactical Communist defeat
Marked the successful overthrow of the South Vietnamese government
Ended U.S. ground combat operations immediately
Led to North Vietnam's surrender
questions.correctText: Eroded American public support for the Vietnam War despite being a tactical Communist defeat

questions.prompt: Betty Friedan's 'The Feminine Mystique' (1963) is most often credited with
choices: Sparking second-wave feminism by critiquing the limits of suburban domesticity
Defending traditional gender roles in postwar America
Founding the National Organization for Women's anti-abortion wing
Endorsing the Equal Rights Amendment's defeat
questions.correctText: Sparking second-wave feminism by critiquing the limits of suburban domesticity

questions.prompt: The Supreme Court's decision in Roe v. Wade (1973) established
choices: A constitutional right to abortion grounded in privacy
Federal restrictions on contraceptive use
State authority to prohibit all abortions
Equal protection under the 19th Amendment
questions.correctText: A constitutional right to abortion grounded in privacy

questions.prompt: Nixon's 1972 visit to China is best understood as part of
choices: A strategy of détente that exploited the Sino-Soviet split to U.S. advantage
An alliance with Mao Zedong against Western Europe
A formal abandonment of containment
A return to Eisenhower-era brinkmanship
questions.correctText: A strategy of détente that exploited the Sino-Soviet split to U.S. advantage

questions.prompt: The Watergate scandal culminated in 1974 with
choices: Richard Nixon's resignation under threat of impeachment
Congressional passage of a constitutional amendment limiting presidential terms
The Supreme Court overturning U.S. v. Nixon
Lyndon Johnson's refusal to seek reelection
questions.correctText: Richard Nixon's resignation under threat of impeachment

questions.prompt: Ronald Reagan's economic program, often called 'Reaganomics,' was based on
choices: Supply-side tax cuts, deregulation, and increased defense spending
Wage and price controls on key industries
Expansion of the New Deal welfare state
Nationalization of the energy sector
questions.correctText: Supply-side tax cuts, deregulation, and increased defense spending

questions.prompt: The fall of the Soviet Union in 1991 most directly resulted in
choices: The end of the Cold War and the emergence of a unipolar U.S.-led international order
An immediate expansion of NATO into Eastern Europe
A formal reunification of North and South Korea
The collapse of the European Union
questions.correctText: The end of the Cold War and the emergence of a unipolar U.S.-led international order

questions.prompt: The North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA), implemented in 1994, most directly aimed to
choices: Eliminate trade barriers between the United States, Canada, and Mexico
Restrict immigration from Latin America
Create a North American common currency
End all tariffs with the European Union
questions.correctText: Eliminate trade barriers between the United States, Canada, and Mexico

questions.prompt: Following the September 11, 2001 attacks, the USA PATRIOT Act expanded federal authority over
choices: Surveillance and law enforcement powers in domestic counterterrorism investigations
Federal income tax collection during wartime
Affirmative action in federal hiring
International trade negotiations
questions.correctText: Surveillance and law enforcement powers in domestic counterterrorism investigations

questions.prompt: The 2008 financial crisis is most often attributed by historians to
choices: Subprime mortgage lending, financial deregulation, and excessive leverage in the housing market
An overproduction of consumer goods in heavy industry
Sudden imposition of high tariffs on Asian imports
Federal Reserve interest rate increases above 15 percent
questions.correctText: Subprime mortgage lending, financial deregulation, and excessive leverage in the housing market

questions.prompt: The Supreme Court's decision in Obergefell v. Hodges (2015) established
choices: A constitutional right to same-sex marriage nationwide
Federal authority over state divorce laws
A national health insurance mandate
The constitutionality of the Defense of Marriage Act
questions.correctText: A constitutional right to same-sex marriage nationwide
